codeReview.summary: Implementation provides pool management, allocation, and cleanup but has a critical bug: after cleanup in release(), the worktree's ready flag is not reset to false, causing is_available() to return true for deleted worktrees. This could cause the pool to allocate non-existent worktrees.

issues.description: In release(), after cleanup_worktree() removes the physical worktree directory, the worktree's ready flag remains true. This causes is_available() to incorrectly return true for a deleted worktree, leading to potential allocation of non-existent worktrees on subsequent allocate() calls.
release() silently succeeds when no allocation exists for the given task_id. While not a bug, it could hide logic errors in callers.

create_worktree_internal always marks worktree as ready=true on 'already exists' case, even if the existing worktree is from a previous failed run. No validation that the directory actually contains a valid worktree.
No test coverage for initialize() - the core async setup method. All 9 tests only test struct creation and synchronous methods.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker used tempfile::tempdir in tests but AGENTS.md/testing conventions aren't documented. The AGENTS.md in the repo has placeholder sections (Build/Test/Coding Style/Testing all say '(To be added...)'), so there's no canonical guidance on testing patterns like temp directories, mocks, etc.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: worktree_pool.rs:351 uses tempfile::tempdir - this is reasonable but not documented

sharedStateObservations.observation: Skill file .factory/skills/worker/SKILL.md references '../swell-core' for imports but the actual crate uses crate::swell_core or just swell_core:: since it's in the workspace. Worker correctly used swell_core:: directly, so the skill may be stale.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: SKILL.md says 'Import from ../swell-core using ../swell-core' but worker used 'use swell_core::AgentId' which is correct for workspace crate

summary: Implementation covers all three expected behaviors (pool of worktrees, allocation per agent, cleanup on completion) but contains a blocking bug: the ready flag is not reset after cleanup, causing released worktrees to still appear available. Tests pass but don't cover the initialize() method or the allocate→release lifecycle. The skill file guidance appears outdated.
